description_long: This gatehouse is constructed primarily from red brick with dark stone or concrete trim, featuring a prominent central archway as the main entry. The facade includes a large, circular window above the arch, flanked by two rectangular windows with gridded panes. The structure is composed of three main segments: a central gabled section, a flat-topped side wall with pilasters, and a curved wall segment, all unified by consistent brickwork and stone detailing. The roof of the central section is a steep gable, while the side elements are flat or gently curved. The overall design suggests a blend of utilitarian and decorative elements, typical of late 19th to early 20th-century public architecture. The open archway allows for passage, and the robust materials imply durability and security.
description: A red brick and stone gatehouse with a central arched entry, circular window, and symmetrical side walls, including a curved segment. The design features a gabled roof and decorative masonry details.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main arched entry, circular window above, and symmetrical side and curved wall segments.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the curved wall segment and the thin profile of the structure.
description_view_2: Top-down view revealing the layout of the three segments: central gabled section, flat side wall, and curved wall.
description_view_3: Opposite side view emphasizing the curvature of the wall and the linear arrangement of the flat side wall.
attribute_entryway_design: Large arched brick entryway with a circular window above.
attribute_window_placement: Two rectangular windows flanking the arch and a circular window above the entry.
